specific_mechanism: selective macroautophagy: cargo is tagged (typically by ubiquitin / receptors such as p62/SQSTM1 and LC3 lipidation), a double-membrane autophagosome forms around it, fuses with the lysosome, and the cargo is degraded; the whole process is induced by nutrient/energy stress via the mTOR-AMPK axis and resolved when nutrient sensing recovers
llm_application: An LLM agent runtime where each piece of internal state (skills, episodic memories, environment models, transient goals) carries a typed degradation tag (analogous to ubiquitin-on-cargo with type-specific receptors), and a budget-stress sensor (analogous to mTOR-AMPK) triggers a 'selective autophagy' cleanup phase whose routing decisions are determined by tag type, not by a single uniform pruning rule; cleanup terminates when budget pressure recovers.
novelty_claim: Existing LLM-agent memory work (A-Mem Ebbinghaus, MemMachine, Intelligent Decay in 2509.25250, MemoryAgentBench selective-forgetting) uses uniform-policy decay or score-based pruning. Selective macroautophagy's distinctive features — typed cargo tagging via receptor adapters, multiple parallel pathways (mitophagy/ER-phagy/pexophagy) keyed on cargo type, and resource-pressure-triggered phase transitions through mTOR-AMPK rather than time-decay — have not, to my knowledge, been imported as an organizing architecture for agent memory management.
